name: Access to Higher Education

history.notes: Initial RAG from HoA Tracker (Meeting 1 baseline). Converted from original scale (original: 5).

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 1 — Initial HoA audit meeting.

Summary: Not great digitally — splitting provision up. Animal, Nursing, Psych, Business. Digital is not a top priority for Sean.

Strengths noted: Not recorded.

AFIs: Meeting not yet arranged. No form submitted.
activityLog.keyDiscussionPoints: Not great digitally — splitting provision up. Animal, Nursing, Psych, Business. Digital is not a top priority for Sean.

actions.text: Explore or chase Digital Skills handover doc from Sean to plan for support.

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 2 — Follow-up audit meeting.

Key Discussion Points: Limited cloud platform training (AWS gap). Staff laptops underpowered for computing needs. Hardware maintenance burden. Digital poverty (learners without home devices).

Strengths: Highly digitally mature. Staff confident with tools & self-directed. Industry-standard equipment (PC, VR, 3D printer, milling machine). Strong outcomes.

AFIs & Next Steps: Phase 1: Ask Sean to mentor one other area (suggest DCI or GMA). AWS training pathway for staff (external CPD). Phase 2: Staff laptop upgrade proposal to leadership. Health Check: Monthly reflective practice with Sean (showcase model).

actions.text: Explore or chase Digital Skills handover doc from Sean to plan for support.

name: Building Services (Construction)
strengths: Clear industry-relevant digital skills vision — clocking in, MS Fundamentals, business start-up. Richard is motivated.

history.notes: Initial RAG from HoA Tracker (Meeting 1 baseline). Converted from original scale (original: 3).

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 1 — Initial HoA audit meeting.

Summary: Idea for clocking in/out resource. Wants to teach MS Fundamentals. Business start-up programme. Clear industry-relevant digital skills vision.

Strengths noted: Clear industry-relevant digital skills vision — clocking in, MS Fundamentals, business start-up. Richard is motivated.

AFIs: No survey form. No Digital Lead identified. Teams environment not assessed.
activityLog.keyDiscussionPoints: Idea for clocking in/out resource. Wants to teach MS Fundamentals. Business start-up programme. Clear industry-relevant digital skills vision.

actions.text: Support Richard with clocking-in resource design. Book MS Fundamentals session. Identify Digital Lead.

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 2 — Follow-up audit meeting.

Key Discussion Points: Inconsistent network & connectivity in workshop areas. Limited learner confidence/independence with digital systems. Access to industry-standard CAD software.

Strengths: Strong foundation in digital portfolio systems and online evidence tracking. Clear progression in digital teaching adoption. Good learner exposure to industry tools.

AFIs & Next Steps: Meet with Richard & DLs (Jakeway, Ross) to review Health Checks. Network infrastructure audit in workshops. Prioritize CAD/simulation software options.
activityLog.keyDiscussionPoints: Inconsistent network & connectivity in workshop areas. Limited learner confidence/independence with digital systems. Access to industry-standard CAD software.

actions.text: Need to sit with Richard and team to flesh out plans for clocking in/out systems, MS Fundamentals, Business Start-Up programmes, how to build a foundation and culture of learning.

name: Games, Media & Animation
strengths: Strong digital TLA already embedded. Gina is open to peer learning and external challenge.

history.notes: Initial RAG from HoA Tracker (Meeting 1 baseline). Converted from original scale (original: 1).

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 1 — Initial HoA audit meeting.

Summary: Gina confident digital foundations are strong. Invited Graeme in to explore and advise.

Strengths noted: Strong digital TLA already embedded. Gina is open to peer learning and external challenge.

AFIs: No form submitted. No formal Digital Lead from GMA identified. Practice not yet documented for wider sharing.
activityLog.keyDiscussionPoints: Gina confident digital foundations are strong. Invited Graeme in to explore and advise.

actions.text: Arrange observation visit. Document and share GMA good practice. Identify Digital Lead from team.

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 2 — Follow-up audit meeting.

Key Discussion Points: WiFi connectivity issues (critical). Digital poverty (home laptop access gaps for some learners). Staff confidence varying in Adobe & specialist software.

Strengths: Digitally rich area with excellent device range (Macs, 3D printers, tablets, VR headsets). Area is inherently digital. Multiple specialist tools embedded.

AFIs & Next Steps: Phase 1: WiFi audit with IT. IT support needs analysis with Gina & Dan. Phase 2: Adobe suite staff training plan. Specialist software skills mapping.

actions.text: Arrange observation visit. Document and share GMA good practice. Identify Digital Lead from team.

name: Hair, Beauty & Hospitality
strengths: Strong embedded digital skills practice. Two Digital Leads identified. Good model for other vocational areas.

history.notes: Initial RAG from HoA Tracker (Meeting 1 baseline). Converted from original scale (original: 1).

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 1 — Initial HoA audit meeting.

Summary: Digital Hours not needed as a formal requirement. Digital skills embedded in portfolios, social media, editing.

Strengths noted: Strong embedded digital skills practice. Two Digital Leads identified. Good model for other vocational areas.

AFIs: Digital hours not formally tracked. No explicit EDS mapping in BP.
activityLog.keyDiscussionPoints: Digital Hours not needed as a formal requirement. Digital skills embedded in portfolios, social media, editing.

actions.text: Support Tracey White as Digital Lead. Help Jenna map existing digital practice to EDS for BP purposes.

activityLog.notes:
Meeting 2 — Follow-up audit meeting.

Key Discussion Points: Software licensing gaps impacting teaching. Limited accessibility training & confidence. No current method for tracking social media analytics. iPad provision issues.

Strengths: Staff confident and in good position to develop. Strong social media focus with industry relevance. Excellent portfolio systems (Padlet).

AFIs & Next Steps: Meet with Jenna, DLs (White) to confirm priorities. Accessibility and Digital Support for Lasting Learning — teach-meet planning. iPad provision review.
activityLog.keyDiscussionPoints: Software licensing gaps impacting teaching. Limited accessibility training & confidence. No current method for tracking social media analytics. iPad provision issues.

actions.text: Reach out to discuss supporting observations and learning walks to see the impact of digital infrastructure on learning delivery.

generalDigitalSkills.currentGaps: Digital hours not formally tracked. No explicit EDS mapping in BP.
